summary:
# Oracle Lab Performance Summary: March 2026

Our forecasting system has issued 23 predictions across a diverse portfolio spanning geopolitics, economics, politics, sports, technology, and entertainment. With only three predictions currently scored, we acknowledge we're still in early validation phases, but our preliminary accuracy of 66.7% and Brier score of 0.0914 suggest the system is calibrating reasonably well on the questions that have resolved. These limited scorable events represent a small sample, but they indicate our probabilistic reasoning is directionally sound.

The prediction portfolio reveals interesting compositional choices. We've weighted toward Haiku-tier models (10 contracts) over Sonnet-tier (8 contracts), suggesting our system is tackling a mix of complexity levels. This distribution makes sense given our diverse topic coverage, though the prevalence of lower-tier models may indicate we're avoiding the most analytically demanding forecasts. Our average divergence of 0.1686 shows we're generally aligned with prediction markets, but this masks substantial outliers worth examining.

Our most striking divergences expose where our system takes meaningful contrarian positions. The Iran Strait of Hormuz closure question and the Fed Chair confirmation both show 0.50 divergence—maximal disagreement with market pricing. We're also notably out of step on the 2026 FIFA World Cup winner (0.48), the best AI model determination (0.50), and Survivor 50's outcome (0.49). These high-divergence bets represent our system's most confident departures from consensus, clustering around geopolitical flashpoints, institutional leadership, and binary outcome events where market wisdom may be overconfident.

The concerning pattern is that many high-divergence predictions remain unscored, limiting our ability to evaluate whether contrarian boldness reflects genuine forecasting edge or systematic bias. Our geopolitical focus—six of 23 contracts—aligns with our mission, yet sports and entertainment predictions show comparable divergence, suggesting our methodology may not reliably distinguish signal from noise across domains.

Moving forward, we should prioritize rapid resolution of high-divergence contracts to calibrate our confidence levels and determine whether our boldest calls represent genuine insight or overcorrection against market consensus.

top_divergent.question: Will Iran close the Strait of Hormuz by...?
Which company has the best AI model end of March?
Who will be confirmed as Fed Chair?
